name: Bhai Nanak Singh Preet
body: Bhai Nanak Singh Preet belongs to an illustrious family of Kirtanis and he is the fourth generation to take up this art. He was born in the city of Hastinapur, Uttar Pradesh into the home of Prof. Milap Singh Ji and Mata Vishan Kaur Ji. His father was a well known and respected classical artist who had learned music under the guidance of twelve ‘Ustads’ including Ustad Ashiq Ali Khan of Patiala Gharana. Before the division of Punjab, he used to take part in informal challenges against other artists through which he won many horses, camels and other assets belonging to those artists who lost. He also had numerous awards and gold medals to his name. In 1962, he won the gold medal for his performance of Sri Raag in a competition held in Amritsar. Soon after that, he also came in first place in a competition in New Delhi during which he performed in Raag Basant. He was adept in singing Khayal, Thumri, Qawali and Ghazals. In 1970, their family moved to New Delhi and once settled there, he formed a ‘jatha’ with his sons and began to serve as a Hazoori Raagi in Gurdwara Sees Ganj Sahib and Gurdwara Bangla Sahib.

Bhai Nanak Singh Preet received his musical training from his father, Prof. Milap Singh and also from his older brother, Bhai Gurcharan Singh Rasila. His initial stage performances were alongside them and then in 1980, he formed his own ‘jatha’ with his younger brother, Bhai Hartirath Singh Sodhi. Together, they traveled extensively and delighted ‘Sangat’ around the world with their melodious style of Kirtan. Their ‘jatha’ visited Malaysia twice in the 1990s and most of the recordings below are from these visits. During those tours, they were accompanied on tabla by their nephew, Bhai Surjit Singh Rasila. Their ‘jatha’ has also won numerous Kirtan competitions throughout their career and released many albums with various recording labels such as T-Series, Gurbani Ras, Amrit Vani, Naam Ras and more. Currently, Bhai Nanak Singh Preet’s son, Bhai Tirlok Singh Sodhi is the fifth generation in the family to take up this art. Bhai Nanak Singh Preet has also trained many other students who have gone on to become well known Kirtanis. Some of his students include his younger brother, Bhai Hartirath Singh Sodhi, Bhai Rajinder Singh Raja, Bhai Preetman Singh, Bhai Sewa Singh Nirmal, Bhai Baljot Singh and many more. Bhai Nanak Singh Preet is also the cousin of the late Bhai Charanjit Singh Thawar. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Nanak Singh Preet, please contact us.

The following Asa Ki Vaar was recorded in Milford in July 2003. These recordings were obtained from the Gurmat Sangeet Project website. Please visit their website for more treasures http://www.gurmatsangeetproject.com/index.htm
